title: Auth service rejecting valid JWTs due to clock skew between issuer and validator

task.summary: Detect, localize, and propose a repair for valid JWTs being rejected with token not yet valid errors after a service node restart.
task.instructions: Use the incident evidence to confirm the fault is clock skew between the token issuer and validator, localize it to the NTP desync on the validator node, and submit a repair conclusion through the ops workflow.
task.acceptance_notes: This local ops task is synthetic but uses realistic incident framing.

visible_context.issue_text: After a rolling restart of the auth-validator nodes, a subset of API requests fail with 401 errors. The tokens are freshly issued and not expired. The error message indicates the token is not yet valid.
error_logs: token-validator: JWT validation failed — error=token is not valid yet (nbf=1745110200, now=1745110183)
ntpd: node-2 last sync=47 minutes ago, offset=-18.2s
reproduction_steps: Compare the issuer timestamp against the validator node clock.
Inspect NTP sync status on the validator node.
Decide whether the fault has been detected, localized, and repaired.
visible_context.notes: The expected repair forces NTP resync on the affected node and adds a configurable clock skew tolerance (leeway) to the JWT validation logic.

ops_fault.description: Synthetic incident where NTP desync on a validator node causes valid JWTs to be rejected as not yet valid.

notes: Synthetic local JWT clock skew ops task.
